title: dense molten canadian minced steak grill
description: tangy molten adventurous canadian dish featuring lean minced steak deglazed with a rich fond, served on a cedar plank.
instructions: season the minced steak with salt pepper and thyme.
brush the cedar plank with vegetable oil and place on the grill to warm up for 2 minutes.
add the minced steak to the plank and cook for 4 minutes per side.
remove the steak from the plank and transfer to a plate, cover with foil to keep warm.
in the same pan, add the garlic and cook for 1 minute until fragrant.
add the red wine vinegar and brown sugar to the pan, scraping up any browned bits with a wooden spoon.
bring the mixture to a simmer and cook for 2 minutes until reduced and syrupy.
add the cup of water to the pan and bring to a simmer.
return the steak to the pan and cook for an additional 2-3 minutes until the internal temperature reaches 130f.
remove the steak from the heat and let rest for 5 minutes before slicing.
serve the sliced steak on top of the cedar plank with the deglazing sauce spooned over the top.
optional: garnish with fresh thyme sprigs and serve immediately.
